Covers: Foundation, source audit, and AI project-manager contract
Goal: Create the cumulative blueprint baseline grounded in the uploaded main flow, previous Wave 00 pack, plans, tracker, advanced additions, and conversation requirements.
Purpose: Give the AI project manager a complete authoritative starting contract before any runtime implementation begins.
Core Requirements: Source inventory; 35-wave schedule; cumulative pack rules; implementation assumptions; AI PM operating rules; QA philosophy; runtime-vs-plan boundaries; deliverable manifest.

Covers: GitHub, local repo, EC2 mirror, and cost-aware development operations
Goal: Move project work into a structured local GitHub-backed repository at C:\Comfy_UI_Main and remote repository KevinSGarrett/Comfy_UI_Main while keeping EC2 off by default.
Purpose: Reduce EC2 cost, make local edits reviewable, and create a safe promotion path from local planning/code to EC2 GPU runtime proof.
Core Requirements: Repo scaffold; .gitignore; model exclusion; Git LFS policy; branch strategy; local CI; EC2 deploy script; rollback plan; secrets separation; no model binaries in Git.

Covers: Model asset storage, S3/EC2/local cache, and manifest registry
Goal: Define how hundreds of GB/TB of model files live outside Git while local workflows still validate references and EC2 can hydrate needed assets on demand.
Purpose: Solve the model-file problem without committing huge binaries or keeping GPU EC2 online unnecessarily.
Core Requirements: S3 canonical model store; EC2 cache; optional local cache; model manifest with SHA256/size/engine/category; hydration/dehydration scripts; missing-model stubs; sync include/exclude; cache eviction policy.

Covers: Runtime inventory, object_info snapshots, and validation harness
Goal: Build validators for ComfyUI workflows, API workflows, nodes, model paths, LoRA references, registries, prompt templates, and generated artifacts.
Purpose: Catch failures locally before expensive EC2 runs.
Core Requirements: Workflow JSON parser; reachable-node analyzer; object_info snapshot comparison; path validation against manifest; disabled/rejected asset blocking; QA schema validation; CI job.

Covers: Main flow deconstruction and active-vs-catalog separation
Goal: Separate the current giant main flow into active production lanes, staged lanes, notes, disabled libraries, and catalog-only model selectors.
Purpose: Stop mistaking canvas presence for runtime effect.
Core Requirements: Reachability report; node group inventory; active SaveImage lane list; catalog extraction; dead/disconnected node list; migration map to modules/subgraphs.

Covers: Workflow modules, subgraphs, API workflow templates, and App Mode interface
Goal: Convert production functions into small reusable workflow modules/subgraphs and expose only safe high-level controls through App Mode.
Purpose: Make the system usable, maintainable, and automatable instead of one unmanageable mega graph.
Core Requirements: Base/refine/mask/detail/contact/video/audio modules; API JSON templates; App Mode input/output contract; UI presets; module smoke tests.

Covers: Engine compatibility registry and pass router
Goal: Create a strict engine router for Flux, SDXL/RealVisXL, Pony, SD1.5, Z-Image, video engines, and audio tools.
Purpose: Use unique models only in compatible passes and prevent cross-engine corruption.
Core Requirements: Engine manifest; LoRA compatibility tags; VAE/CLIP rules; image bridge rules; specialty pass permissions; disabled/rejected model enforcement.

Covers: LLM Scene Director, scene request schema, and autonomous plan generation
Goal: Turn natural scene requests into structured scene, character, shot, pass, mask, camera, environment, video, and audio plans.
Purpose: Allow the AI system to decide what to generate without manual node-by-node configuration.
Core Requirements: Scene parser; clarification rules; structured JSON output; preset selection; plan validation; task decomposition; generation budget estimate.

Covers: Character Bible, identity registry, and per-character reference packs
Goal: Define character identity, face, body, skin, hair, clothing, voice, and continuity references per character.
Purpose: Make characters look consistent and intentionally designed across images, video, and audio.
Core Requirements: Character IDs; reference hashing; FaceID/IPAdapter strategy; body-shape references; outfit states; voice IDs; identity QA; drift repair plan.

Covers: Environment, world, lighting, material, and prop realism system
Goal: Add structured environments, room geometry, lighting rigs, props, furniture, surfaces, reflections, scale, and environmental state.
Purpose: Make scenes feel physically located instead of floating studio prompts.
Core Requirements: Environment Bible; room/furniture masks; lighting presets; surface material states; prop registry; environment QA; scale/depth consistency checks.

Covers: Camera, lens, zoom, depth, angle, framing, and shot planner
Goal: Build a camera system for angle, lens, distance, crop, depth of field, zoom, POV, full-body/half-body framing, and multi-character framing.
Purpose: Fix camera/framing as a controlled shot-design problem, not a vague prompt.
Core Requirements: Shot schema; camera profile library; framing templates; full-body/half-body/headshot validators; multi-character in-frame QA; lens/depth/zoom tags.

Covers: Pose, action, body blocking, and camera-aware control maps
Goal: Build pose and action control through DWPose/OpenPose, depth, normal, lineart, Canny, segmentation, and camera-aware contact visibility.
Purpose: Make pose and action accuracy geometrically controlled.
Core Requirements: Pose map generation; per-character skeletons; hand keypoints; depth/normal; action blocking; pose QA; camera/pose consistency.

Covers: Frame composition and subject-count integrity
Goal: Ensure requested characters and body extents are correctly visible: full body, half body, close-up, multiple full bodies, foreground/background placement.
Purpose: Prevent missing limbs, cropped bodies, merged bodies, wrong character count, and wrong subject scale.
Core Requirements: Frame planner; character count detector; body-extents QA; crop-boundary warnings; out-of-frame detection; reframe/rerun rules.

Covers: Ultimate Mask Factory: macro, major, minor, micro, and nano masks
Goal: Create the full mask hierarchy for people, body parts, clothes, environment, contact zones, pores, blemishes, pressure marks, and fine details.
Purpose: Make every detail pass spatially truthful and prevent chopped-body artifacts.
Core Requirements: Person instance masks; major body masks; minor body-region masks; micro/nano texture masks; mask feather/grow rules; mask overlays; mask QA.

Covers: Autonomous pass planner, workflow compiler, and ComfyUI API orchestrator
Goal: Build the controller that decides pass order, patches workflow JSON, runs ComfyUI locally/EC2, reads outputs, QA scores, and schedules reruns.
Purpose: Make the system actually know when to run first, second, third, detail, repair, video, and audio passes.
Core Requirements: Pass-plan schema; state machine; ComfyUI /prompt integration; local dry-runs; EC2 GPU proof mode; retry/rerun rules; output manifests.

Covers: Image base generation lanes
Goal: Create reliable Flux-first, SDXL/RealVisXL, Z-Image, Pony specialty, and fallback base generation lanes.
Purpose: Generate correct composition, character count, camera, pose, silhouette, and environment before detail.
Core Requirements: Base lane templates; low global LoRA limits; reference inputs; control maps; seed strategy; base QA gate.

Covers: Image refine, specialty engine passes, and engine bridging
Goal: Create full-image refine and masked specialty passes that bridge Flux outputs into SDXL/Pony/RealVisXL/etc. through decoded images.
Purpose: Use each engine for what it is good at without mixing incompatible model graphs.
Core Requirements: VAE encode/decode bridges; low-denoise settings; identity preservation; before/after QA; engine-specific prompt adapters.

Covers: Body shape, silhouette, and proportion correction
Goal: Add large-mask correction for stomach, waist, hips, thighs, butt, shoulders, torso, height/scale, and full silhouette.
Purpose: Fix body shape without chopped pasted regions.
Core Requirements: Large mask recipes; depth/edge guidance; body measurement QA; rerun-base vs inpaint decision rules; silhouette overlays.

Covers: Skin/material realism, blemishes, pores, cellulite, sweat, oil, and pressure marks
Goal: Add stateful surface realism with localized detail passes for skin and body features.
Purpose: Make realism details appear only where intended and persist across shots.
Core Requirements: Skin/material state ledger; small/medium masks; cellulite thigh passes; pores/blemishes; sweat/oil; pressure marks; naturalness QA.

Covers: Clothing, fabric, props, furniture, and environment contact realism
Goal: Add fabric folds, compression, stretch, cling, wrinkles, wetness, prop interaction, furniture deformation, and body support marks.
Purpose: Make clothing and environment react believably to bodies and motion.
Core Requirements: Clothing masks; fabric state; prop masks; furniture support graph; wrinkle/contact QA; environment continuity.

Covers: Face, hands, feet, eyes, teeth, nails, and hard anatomy detailers
Goal: Build strict crop/detail repair lanes for difficult anatomy.
Purpose: Stop rough hands/faces by using dedicated detectors and local repair passes.
Core Requirements: Face crop; hand crop; hand keypoints; finger count; nails; eyes/teeth; feet; crop QA; no global hand LoRAs by default.

Covers: Soft-body material profiles and mechanics library
Goal: Define firmness/softness/sag/bounce/ripple/jiggle profiles for body regions and materials.
Purpose: Give the system structured visual targets for soft-body behavior without claiming true physics simulation.
Core Requirements: Region profiles; firmness scale; sag profiles; compression/rebound hints; side-to-side/hanging behavior; mechanics presets; QA evidence requirements.

Covers: Physical interaction contact graph
Goal: Create a contact graph that binds source body parts, target body parts, pressure, visibility, occlusion, intensity, duration, and expected audio force.
Purpose: Make body/hand/prop interactions explicit and machine-checkable.
Core Requirements: Source/target region binding; pressure maps; collision zones; occlusion priority; multi-character contact edges; contact QA.

Covers: Deformation, indentation, collision, compression, pull/push, and visual soft-mesh passes
Goal: Implement masked contact-detail passes that create believable visual pressure, finger indentions, squeeze, pull, push, compression, and deformation.
Purpose: Turn soft-body/contact specs into local generated visual evidence.
Core Requirements: Hand/body/contact masks; falloff masks; depth/normal; contact prompts; denoise ranges; before/after contact crops; reject if merged/melted.

Covers: Multi-character instance layout and separation
Goal: Build 1–5 character scene layouts with per-character masks, prompt regions, identity refs, depth order, and frame placement.
Purpose: Prevent multiple characters from collapsing into one body.
Core Requirements: Person A/B/C/D/E masks; per-character references; skeletons; layout QA; merge detection; per-instance pass routing.

Covers: Multi-character interactions, occlusion, and choreography
Goal: Make multiple characters interact with correct pose, body contact, hand placement, object contact, and occlusion.
Purpose: Achieve realistic group posing without fused limbs or conflicting identities.
Core Requirements: Interaction graph; occlusion layers; per-character contact edges; cross-character masks; interaction QA; targeted rerun rules.

Covers: GIF/video keyframe, shot, and timeline planner
Goal: Generate still keyframes, pose/depth/mask timelines, motion states, GIF loops, and video shot plans.
Purpose: Make motion controllable even without reference video or 3D simulation.
Core Requirements: Keyframe schema; frame states; mask interpolation; GIF/MP4/WebM outputs; storyboard preview; frame QA.

Covers: Video engine routing, temporal consistency, frame repair, and stitching
Goal: Route to Wan/Hunyuan/LTXV/AnimateDiff-style lanes and repair temporal errors.
Purpose: Convert still-image control into usable video/GIF production.
Core Requirements: Video engine registry; per-frame manifests; identity/pose/flicker QA; optical-flow checks; bad-frame repair; stitch boundary QA.

Covers: Micro-motion and secondary motion layer
Goal: Add breathing, tremble, grip changes, gaze, hair motion, chest/abdomen movement, ripple, bounce, and subtle secondary motion.
Purpose: Make bodies feel alive across GIF/video outputs.
Core Requirements: Micro-motion tracks; region motion curves; phase alignment; soft-body secondary motion schedules; temporal QA.

Covers: Long-form continuity, fatigue, variation, and scene-state memory
Goal: Track long-form body state, fatigue, pacing, state changes, repetition control, clothing state, marks, fluids, and continuity.
Purpose: Prevent scenes from resetting or repeating unnaturally.
Core Requirements: Scene-state ledger; body-state timeline; fatigue curves; variation rules; continuity QA; shot-to-shot state diff.

Covers: Audio generation: voice, dialogue, breathing, foley, ambience, music, and SFX
Goal: Build audio lanes linked to the scene graph, character IDs, action timeline, and environment.
Purpose: Make audio generated as part of the same production plan instead of an afterthought.
Core Requirements: Voice profiles; dialogue; breathing; foley; impacts; ambience; music beds; clipping/intelligibility QA; manifests.

Covers: Pose-to-audio force model, spatial audio, and room acoustics
Goal: Map visual action/contact force to synchronized sound design and spatial mixing.
Purpose: Make audio match contact, motion, room size, camera distance, and actor placement.
Core Requirements: Audio force map; room impulse profile; panning; reverb; distance; sync QA; force mismatch detection.

Covers: State diff debugger, revision manager, variants, and learning loop
Goal: Compare planned state, compiled state, generated state, QA-observed state, and revised state; manage takes and successful-run presets.
Purpose: Make iterative improvement targeted and measurable.
Core Requirements: State diff categories; take IDs; variant recipes; failure clustering; preset saving; successful-run learning; targeted reruns.

Covers: Proxy preview, animatics, realism budgets, and cost control
Goal: Create cheap previews before final EC2 GPU runs and assign realism budgets to passes.
Purpose: Avoid wasting EC2 money on bad scene logic.
Core Requirements: Text preview; storyboard; pose/contact animatic; rough motion; scratch audio; budget modes; escalation to final render only after preview QA.

Covers: Final integration, App Mode release, EC2 proof, QA certification, and cumulative handoff
Goal: Assemble the full still/GIF/video/audio hyper-realism system and produce final release evidence.
Purpose: Deliver an AI-operable end-to-end production system with strict QA and reproducible deployment.
Core Requirements: End-to-end tests; local+EC2 proofs; App Mode UI; docs; schemas; manifests; final QA certification; release bundle.
